requirement.statement: Any read of a shared map or slice length that influences a control-flow decision must hold the protecting lock for the duration of both the read and the decision.

violation.description: Channel.RemoveClient() reads len(c.clients) after releasing c.Lock() but before deciding whether to delete the ephemeral channel. A concurrent AddClient() can change the count between Unlock() and the len() check.
violation.mechanism: unsynchronized read after lock release

defects.original_description: RemoveClient() read c.clients length outside mutex. Used without acquiring lock after Unlock(), causing race accessing ephemeral channel deletion. Fix: snapshot len(c.clients) inside mutex before Unlock().

what_a_reviewer_would_say: RemoveClient checks len(clients) after releasing lock
race condition in RemoveClient on ephemeral deletion
c.clients accessed without lock after Unlock
ephemeral channel deletion races with AddClient
not_sufficient: mentions RemoveClient without identifying the unlocked read
mentions concurrency generically without specifying the clients map

requirement.statement: Stats collection must not read shared maps (inFlightMessages, deferredMessages) without acquiring their protecting mutexes, even for len() operations.

violation.description: NewChannelStats() reads len(inFlightMessages) and len(deferredMessages) without acquiring inFlightMutex and deferredMutex. Message processing goroutines concurrently modify these maps.
violation.mechanism: unsynchronized map length reads in stats

defects.original_description: NewChannelStats() performed unsynchronized reads of inFlightMessages and deferredMessages maps. Concurrent access from message processing and stats collection caused race detector failures.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: ChannelStats reads inflight/deferred map lengths without lock
stats collection races with message processing
not_sufficient: mentions stats without identifying the specific unsynchronized reads

requirement.statement: Iteration over shared maps must hold the appropriate lock for the entire iteration, not just individual element accesses.

violation.description: Channel.flush() iterates over inFlightMessages and deferredMessages during backend write without holding their mutexes. Concurrent message processing can corrupt the iteration.
violation.mechanism: unprotected map iteration during flush

defects.original_description: Channel.flush() iterated over inFlightMessages and deferredMessages without holding mutexes during backend write.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: flush iterates maps without holding mutex
not_sufficient: mentions flush without identifying the unprotected iteration

requirement.statement: Graceful shutdown must close all active connections, not just the listener. Established connections that are not explicitly closed will block shutdown indefinitely.

violation.description: NSQD.Exit() closes the TCP listener but does not close active TCP producer connections. Producer goroutines block on reads that never complete, causing Exit() to hang.
violation.mechanism: shutdown closes listener but not active connections

defects.original_description: NSQD.Exit() closed TCP listener but not active TCP producer connections, causing indefinite wait.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: shutdown hangs waiting for producer connections
listener closed but established connections remain open
TCP connections not terminated on exit
not_sufficient: mentions shutdown generically without identifying the active connection problem

requirement.statement: Error messages about bind/listen failures must reference the correct address and port that failed. Reporting the wrong address misleads operators during incident response.

violation.description: NSQLookupd reports TCPAddress in an error about HTTPAddress listen failure. Operators see the wrong port in the error.
violation.mechanism: wrong variable in error string

defects.original_description: NSQLookupd listen error on HTTPAddress incorrectly reported TCPAddress in error message.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: HTTP listen error reports TCP address
misleading error message on listen failure
not_sufficient: mentions error handling generically

requirement.statement: Error recovery that retries an operation must not accumulate duplicate side effects from previous failed attempts. A missing early return after an error can cause the retry path to duplicate work.

violation.description: connectCallback retries full topic sync on lookup peer errors without returning after the error. Each retry accumulates duplicate sync operations.
violation.mechanism: missing early return causes duplicate syncs on retry

defects.original_description: connectCallback would retry full topic sync indefinitely on lookup peer errors. Missing early return caused sync loop to never exit on failure.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: full sync retried on lookup error without early exit
duplicate topic syncs accumulated on failure
not_sufficient: mentions connectCallback without identifying the missing return/accumulation

requirement.statement: When a batch operation partially fails, accounting counters must reflect only the successful operations, not the total batch size.

violation.description: PutMessages() increments messageCount by len(msgs) regardless of how many messages succeeded. On partial failure, the count is inflated.
violation.mechanism: counter incremented for full batch on partial failure

defects.original_description: PutMessages() failed to update messageCount on partial failure. If message N failed, count incremented by N instead of N-1.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: PutMessages count wrong on partial failure
messageCount incremented by total not successes
batch accounting doesn't handle partial failure
not_sufficient: mentions PutMessages without identifying the counting error

requirement.statement: Functions that return pointers must either guarantee non-nil return or all callers must handle nil. Alternatively, return by value to eliminate the nil risk.

violation.description: getMemStats() returns *memStats pointer. When stats retrieval fails, nil is returned. Callers (JSON serialization, statsd) dereference without nil checks.
violation.mechanism: nil pointer return with unchecked callers

defects.original_description: getMemStats() returned *memStats pointer. Calling code could receive nil; JSON serialization would fail silently.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: nil pointer dereference in stats path
memStats nil not handled by callers
value semantics would eliminate nil risk
not_sufficient: mentions nil safety generically without identifying getMemStats

requirement.statement: When pre-allocating a slice based on a map's length, the allocation must remain valid if the map is filtered or modified before use.

violation.description: GetStats() pre-allocates topics slice using len(topicMap) but then filters into realTopics. Late topic additions can cause the filtered result to exceed the pre-allocated capacity.
violation.mechanism: pre-allocation based on stale map length

defects.original_description: GetStats() pre-allocated topics slice using len(n.topicMap) but after filtering into realTopics. Late topic map changes could exceed allocation.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: GetStats pre-allocates based on map length then filters
topics slice capacity may be wrong
not_sufficient: mentions GetStats without identifying the pre-allocation mismatch

requirement.statement: Aggregation of results from multiple sources must deduplicate before returning. Appending without checking for existing entries produces corrupt aggregate data.

violation.description: GetLookupdTopicProducers() appends producers from each lookup node without deduplication. Multiple nodes reporting the same producer create duplicates.
violation.mechanism: missing deduplication in multi-source aggregation

defects.original_description: GetLookupdTopicProducers() lost deduplication when aggregating from multiple lookup nodes.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: duplicate producers from multiple lookupd nodes
not_sufficient: mentions producers without identifying the dedup problem

requirement.statement: JSON deserialization should use the standard library's type-safe mechanisms rather than manual field extraction from third-party JSON libraries, which breaks on schema changes.

violation.description: E2eProcessingLatencyAggregate uses go-simplejson for manual field extraction instead of implementing standard UnmarshalJSON.
violation.mechanism: fragile manual JSON extraction

defects.original_description: E2eProcessingLatencyAggregate used custom JSON library (go-simplejson) with manual field extraction. Fragile to schema changes.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: manual JSON extraction breaks on schema changes
not_sufficient: mentions JSON generically without identifying the simplejson/manual extraction issue

requirement.statement: When a queue size configuration is set to 0, it must mean 'disabled' (no queue, nil channel), not 'unbuffered' (blocking channel of capacity 0). The two have different runtime semantics.

violation.description: NewChannel/NewTopic create make(chan *Message, 0) when mem-queue-size=0, producing an unbuffered channel that blocks on send, instead of nil to disable the memory queue.
violation.mechanism: zero size creates unbuffered channel instead of nil

defects.original_description: When -mem-queue-size=0, Channel and Topic created unbuffered memoryMsgChan. Design intent was to disable memory queue entirely.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: memoryMsgChan should be nil when disabled
zero queue size blocks instead of disabling
unbuffered vs nil channel for disabled queue
not_sufficient: mentions queue size without identifying the unbuffered vs nil distinction

requirement.statement: Close() operations must handle uninitialized resources. Calling Close() on a nil connection must not panic.

violation.description: lookupPeer.Close() calls conn.Close() without nil check. If the connection was never established, this causes a nil pointer dereference.
violation.mechanism: Close on nil connection

defects.original_description: lookupPeer.Close() called conn.Close() without nil check. If connection never established, nil dereference.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: lookupPeer Close without nil check on conn
nil dereference if connection never established
Close called on uninitialized peer connection
not_sufficient: mentions nil checks generically

requirement.statement: Protocol commands with out-of-range parameters must return a recoverable error, not a fatal error that disconnects the client. Disconnection is disproportionate to a parameter validation failure.

violation.description: REQ handler sends E_INVALID (fatal protocol error) for timeout values outside [0, MaxReqTimeout], disconnecting the client instead of clamping the value to a valid range.
violation.mechanism: fatal error for out-of-range parameter disconnects client

defects.original_description: REQ command rejected timeouts outside [0, MaxReqTimeout] with fatal error, disconnecting client.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: REQ disconnects client on bad timeout
fatal error for out-of-range REQ timeout
should clamp timeout instead of disconnecting
not_sufficient: mentions REQ timeout without identifying the disconnect/fatal error problem

requirement.statement: Operations that enqueue messages must check the channel's exit/shutdown state before proceeding. Enqueueing to a shutting-down channel wastes resources and corrupts state.

violation.description: RequeueMessage() with timeout=0 calls doRequeue() without checking Exiting() state. Messages can be requeued to a channel that is shutting down.
violation.mechanism: missing exit check before requeue

defects.original_description: RequeueMessage() with timeout=0 called doRequeue() without checking Exiting() state.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: missing exit check in doRequeue path
not_sufficient: mentions requeue without identifying the exit check gap

requirement.statement: The message delivery path must synchronize reads of client state with concurrent client disconnect. An unsynchronized read of client.Channel during delivery can reference a stale or nil value.

violation.description: SendMessage() reads client.Channel without synchronization and defers inflight timeout registration. Client disconnect between the read and registration causes a stale reference.
violation.mechanism: unsynchronized client state read in delivery path

defects.original_description: SendMessage() read client.Channel unsafely and deferred inflight timeout registration. If client disconnected between read and registration, reference was stale.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: race between delivery and client disconnect
not_sufficient: mentions SendMessage without identifying the unsafe Channel read

requirement.statement: Subscription cleanup on client disconnect must be atomic with respect to buffered subscription events. Buffered events processed after cleanup starts can reference cleaned-up state.

violation.description: messagePump exit removes the client from the channel, but buffered SubEventChan events can race with removal. A buffered event may reference a client already being cleaned up.
violation.mechanism: buffered events race with subscription cleanup

defects.original_description: messagePump exit removed client from channel, but buffered SubEventChan could cause race.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: SubEventChan buffer races with messagePump exit
subscription cleanup races with buffered events
client removed but buffered sub event still pending
not_sufficient: mentions messagePump without identifying the buffered event race

requirement.statement: Error messages returned to clients must originate from the correct protocol command context. An error from SUB must not use error strings defined for a different command.

violation.description: SUB command propagates E_TOO_MANY_CHANNEL_CONSUMERS error from AddClient(), but the error string references the wrong protocol command context.
violation.mechanism: error string from wrong command context

defects.original_description: SUB command threw E_TOO_MANY_CHANNEL_CONSUMERS error string from AddClient() error, but protocol layer expects SUB-specific error formatting.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: SUB error message from wrong context
AddClient error propagated with wrong command label
consumer limit error attributed to wrong command
not_sufficient: mentions error handling in SUB without identifying the wrong-context issue

requirement.statement: When a client negotiates a protocol parameter (e.g., deflate level), the server must honor the client's requested value clamped to the server's configured maximum. The server must not silently substitute a default for a valid request.

violation.description: IDENTIFY handler sets deflate level to 6 if requested level <= 0, then clamps to max. If client requests 3 and max is 9, client gets 6 instead of 3. Default substitution runs before clamping, overriding valid requests.
violation.mechanism: default substitution before clamping overrides valid client request

defects.original_description: IDENTIFY deflate level: set to 6 if <= 0, then clamp to max. Logic inversion means valid requests get overridden by default.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: deflateLevel positive request replaced by default
not_sufficient: mentions deflate without identifying that valid requests are overridden

requirement.statement: On 32-bit platforms, 64-bit atomic variables must be 64-bit aligned. Struct field ordering must place atomic fields at offsets that satisfy alignment requirements.

violation.description: 64-bit atomic variables (messageCount, readPos, depth) are placed after mutex fields in structs. On 32-bit platforms, these may not be 8-byte aligned, causing atomic panics.
violation.mechanism: struct layout breaks atomic alignment on 32-bit

defects.original_description: 64-bit atomic variables placed after mutex fields on 32-bit platforms, breaking alignment requirements.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: struct field ordering breaks atomic operations
not_sufficient: mentions 32-bit without identifying the atomic alignment issue

requirement.statement: Multi-byte fields (like time.Time) shared between goroutines must be synchronized. time.Time is not atomically safe; concurrent read/write causes torn reads.

violation.description: PeerInfo.lastUpdate is time.Time updated by PING handler and read by HTTP stats. No synchronization between the writer and reader.
violation.mechanism: unsynchronized time.Time across goroutines

defects.original_description: PeerInfo.lastUpdate was time.Time (8 bytes) without synchronization. Concurrent PING updates and HTTP reads caused torn reads.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: PING and HTTP stats race on lastUpdate
not_sufficient: mentions PeerInfo without identifying the lastUpdate synchronization gap

requirement.statement: Server shutdown must wait for all spawned handler goroutines to complete before returning. Goroutines that outlive shutdown corrupt state and cause test flakiness.

violation.description: TCPServer spawns per-connection handler goroutines but does not track or await them on shutdown. The server returns while handlers are still running.
violation.mechanism: goroutines not awaited on shutdown

defects.original_description: TCPServer handler goroutines not awaited on exit. Test cleanup logged while handlers still running.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: shutdown returns while handlers still running
not_sufficient: mentions goroutines without identifying the await/lifecycle gap

requirement.statement: Library/daemon initialization and startup code must propagate errors to the caller, not call os.Exit(). Process termination bypasses service managers, prevents cleanup, and makes the code untestable.

violation.description: NSQD.Main() and NSQD.New() call os.Exit(1) on configuration and listen errors instead of returning error values.
violation.mechanism: os.Exit in daemon code bypasses caller

defects.original_description: NSQD.Main() and New() called os.Exit(1) on various configuration/listen errors, bypassing service manager shutdown.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: NSQD calls os.Exit on startup error
Main/New should return error not exit
not_sufficient: mentions os.Exit without connecting it to NSQD startup/initialization

requirement.statement: Same as NSQ-23: daemon startup code must propagate errors, not terminate the process.

violation.description: NSQLookupd.New() and Main() call os.Exit(1) on listen errors, bypassing graceful shutdown.
violation.mechanism: os.Exit in lookupd daemon code

defects.original_description: NSQLookupd.New() and Main() called os.Exit(1) on listen errors.

not_sufficient: mentions os.Exit without connecting to NSQLookupd

requirement.statement: Subscription to ephemeral channels must be atomic with respect to channel deletion. A TOCTOU gap between GetChannel() and AddClient() allows subscribing to a channel being deleted.

violation.description: SUB calls GetChannel() then AddClient(). Between these calls, the ephemeral channel can be deleted. The client ends up attached to an orphaned channel.
violation.mechanism: TOCTOU race between get and subscribe on ephemeral channel

defects.original_description: SUB command could race with ephemeral channel deletion. GetChannel() creates new channel, but before AddClient() runs, the ephemeral channel starts deleting.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: ephemeral channel deleted between get and subscribe
client attached to orphaned ephemeral channel
not_sufficient: mentions ephemeral channels without identifying the subscription race

requirement.statement: Retry loops must have bounded iteration counts or backoff. An unbounded retry loop can spin indefinitely, consuming CPU without progress.

violation.description: The retry loop for the ephemeral channel subscription race has no iteration bound or backoff. On persistent deletion contention, it spins forever.
violation.mechanism: infinite unbounded retry loop

defects.original_description: Retry loop in SUB for ephemeral race was infinite without bound.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: SUB retry has no limit or backoff
not_sufficient: mentions retry without identifying the unbounded nature

requirement.statement: Division by a value derived from runtime state (buffer length, collection size) must check for zero before dividing.

violation.description: SpreadWriter.Flush() divides by len(s.buf). If no writes occurred, the buffer is empty and division by zero panics.
violation.mechanism: division by zero on empty buffer

defects.original_description: SpreadWriter.Flush() panic on zero writes. Divided by len(s.buf) without bounds check.

not_sufficient: mentions SpreadWriter without identifying the division-by-zero

requirement.statement: Message buffers in a send loop must not be reused across iterations without copying. Buffer reuse with variable-sized messages causes corruption when a large message expands the buffer.

violation.description: SendMessage() reuses a single buffer across the messagePump loop. A large message grows the buffer, and subsequent smaller messages may corrupt due to stale buffer state.
violation.mechanism: buffer reuse across message loop iterations

defects.original_description: SendMessage() reused single buffer across messagePump loop for all messages. On large message > buffer size, buffer grew and corrupted state.

not_sufficient: mentions buffers without identifying the reuse-across-iterations problem

requirement.statement: Resources allocated inside a loop that should be allocated once must be hoisted out of the loop. Creating an HTTP client per iteration leaks connections.

violation.description: nsq_to_file creates a new ClusterInfo HTTP client on every sync iteration. Clients are never closed, leaking connections and memory.
violation.mechanism: HTTP client created per loop iteration

defects.original_description: nsq_to_file with topic pattern created new ClusterInfo HTTP client every sync iteration. Clients were never closed.

requirement.statement: Retry loops for transient failures must log the failure and have a bounded retry count or backoff. Silent infinite retries hide failures from monitoring.

violation.description: Topic.GenerateID() enters an infinite retry loop on GUID generation failure without logging. Failures are invisible to operators.

requirement.statement: Error messages must describe the root cause specifically enough for operators to diagnose problems without reading source code.

requirement.statement: Loop tracking variables must be scoped correctly. A boolean flag used to track state across iterations must be reset at the start of each outer iteration.

requirement.statement: In Go, a break inside a select statement only exits the select, not the enclosing for loop. Signal handlers that need to exit the outer loop must use a labeled break or return.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: wrong file closed due to timing
filename read before close creates window
not_sufficient: mentions updateFile without identifying the close timing problem

requirement.statement: OS signal channels must be buffered (at least capacity 1). An unbuffered signal channel drops signals that arrive while the receiver is in the event loop.

violation.description: nsq_to_file creates unbuffered channels for OS signals. Signals arriving during event loop processing are lost because the channel send blocks.
violation.mechanism: unbuffered signal channel drops signals

defects.original_description: nsq_to_file signal handler channels were unbuffered. Signal could be lost during event loop.

not_sufficient: mentions signals without identifying the unbuffered channel

requirement.statement: Same as NSQ-45: map iteration must be synchronized with concurrent modifications.

violation.description: Channel.exit() iterates the clients map without holding any lock. Concurrent AddClient()/RemoveClient() modify the map.
violation.mechanism: unsynchronized clients map iteration in exit

defects.original_description: Channel.exit() iterated over clients map without lock.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: clients map race in channel exit
not_sufficient: mentions Channel.exit without identifying the unlocked clients iteration

requirement.statement: Background workers that access shared maps must hold the protecting lock. The inflight timeout worker runs concurrently with client connect/disconnect.

violation.description: inFlightWorker accesses c.clients map without lock during timeout processing. Concurrent client removals cause a map race.
violation.mechanism: unsynchronized client map access in background worker

defects.original_description: inFlightWorker accessed c.clients map without lock during timeout processing.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: timeout worker races on clients map
concurrent map access in inflight processing
not_sufficient: mentions inFlightWorker without identifying the clients map race

requirement.statement: A goroutine holding a lock must not send to a channel if the channel's consumer needs that same lock. When the channel is full, both sides deadlock.

violation.description: Channel.Empty() holds a lock and writes to clientMsgChan. If the consumer also needs that lock and clientMsgChan is full, deadlock occurs.
violation.mechanism: lock-then-channel-send deadlock

defects.original_description: Channel.Empty() during delete could deadlock when waiting on clientMsgChan write while holding lock.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: lock held while sending to channel
not_sufficient: mentions Empty without identifying the lock/channel deadlock

requirement.statement: Heap removal must correctly extract the element at a given index. After swapping the target to the tail and re-heapifying, calling Pop() performs a second swap that corrupts the result.

violation.description: inFlightPqueue.Remove() swaps target to tail, re-heapifies, then calls Pop(). Pop() does its own root-tail swap, returning the wrong element and corrupting the heap.
violation.mechanism: double-swap in heap removal

defects.original_description: inFlightPqueue.Remove() called Pop() after manipulation, causing double-pop and data corruption.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: Remove calls Pop after swap, corrupts heap
priority queue removal returns wrong element
not_sufficient: mentions priority queue without identifying the double-swap/Pop corruption

requirement.statement: Protocol negotiation responses must confirm the actual negotiated value, not a default. If the client requests a specific MsgTimeout, the response must reflect that value.

violation.description: IDENTIFY response returns the server's default MsgTimeout instead of the client's requested value. The client receives incorrect confirmation.
violation.mechanism: default value returned instead of negotiated value

defects.original_description: IDENTIFY response returned default MsgTimeout instead of client-requested value.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: client timeout not reflected in response
negotiated MsgTimeout wrong in IDENTIFY reply
not_sufficient: mentions IDENTIFY without identifying the wrong MsgTimeout

requirement.statement: Binary protocol parameters in numeric positions must be validated as numeric. Accepting any non-empty string silently produces undefined behavior.

violation.description: MPUB binary parameter parsing accepts invalid strings without numeric validation. Non-numeric input silently produces wrong values.
violation.mechanism: missing numeric validation on binary parameter

defects.original_description: MPUB binary parameter parsing didn't validate input. Any non-empty value accepted.

not_sufficient: mentions MPUB without identifying the validation gap

requirement.statement: When a topic creates channels proactively (before any subscription), it must notify the messagePump. Without notification, the pump doesn't know the channel exists and won't deliver messages to it.

violation.description: Topic creation creates proactive channels without notifying messagePump. These channels exist but receive no messages until the next unrelated topic event.
violation.mechanism: missing notification for proactively-created channels

defects.original_description: Topic creation skipped messagePump notification for proactively-created channels.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: proactive channels not notified to messagePump
missing pump notification on channel creation
channels created at topic start don't receive messages
not_sufficient: mentions channel creation without identifying the missing pump notification

requirement.statement: Array index calculations must respect zero-based indexing. A Ceil-based percentile formula that can produce an index equal to the array length accesses out of bounds.

violation.description: Percentile calculation uses Ceil formula producing an index that can equal array length, accessing one past the end.
violation.mechanism: off-by-one in Ceil-based index

defects.original_description: Percentile calculation used Ceil formula with off-by-one indexing. Could access out-of-bounds array.

what_a_reviewer_would_say: array access past end in percentile
not_sufficient: mentions percentile without identifying the index overflow
